mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and GDP signals strong supply-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.05 for any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day returns in co-expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through higher discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in CPI inflation above 240 signals persistent price pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through central bank hawkishness.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in payroll employment combined with rising unemployment signals weak labor market dynamics, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.05 for any 3-year rolling window, or 5-day returns in co-contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in housing starts above 1,000 thousand units signals strong demand for residential construction, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP and consumer spending (RSAFS) signals robust demand conditions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through elevated corporate revenues.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.05 for any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day returns in co-expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
